design_philosophy.core_loop: Pilot the Sol-Feace starship through six horizontally-scrolling stages, destroying enemies and bosses while collecting power-ups to enhance weapons; dual cannon positioning adds tactical layer to classic shooting mechanics
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Collision avoidance against dense enemy projectiles combined with resource-scarcity of power-ups; dual-cannon aiming adds moment-to-moment decision-making
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Command a heavily-armed spacecraft with customizable firing patterns to prevent a rogue supercomputer from enslaving humanity
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Dual-cannon system with diagonal firing and parallel arrangement options represents a moderately novel twist on horizontal shmup conventions, though critics noted the overall design borrowed heavily from established genre templates without substantial innovation

difficulty.checkpointing: Stage-based progression with boss encounters at end of each stage that must be defeated to advance
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Power-up collection provides weapon variety (homing missiles, piercing laser, wide shot) allowing players to adapt to encountered threats; dual-cannon positioning offers tactical flexibility

progression.upgrade_system: Power capsule system granting temporary weapon enhancements: homing missiles, piercing laser, wide-shot capability; upgrades collected from defeated enemies
progression.unlock_mechanism: Stage-based unlock: defeating boss at end of each stage progresses to next. Six stages total culminating in final supercomputer boss
replayability_hooks: Variable power-up routing creates different weapon loadouts per run
Dual-cannon positioning strategy provides tactical variation
Stage design rewards high-score chasing through arcade-style scoring

world_building_techniques: Animated cutscenes between stages explaining plot progression (Sega CD version adds full voice acting)
Stage theming establishes setting: mechanical bases, destroyed star vessels, Jupiter location
Boss encounters serve as narrative gates, each representing obstacles to stopping the supercomputer threat

boss_design.philosophy: Each stage ends with a mandatory boss fight representing an obstacle in the journey to stop the supercomputer antagonist. Bosses feature identifiable weak points that must be targeted for damage, combining pattern recognition with aiming precision
notable_bosses: Stage 1 boss (X68000 screenshot documents first encounter)

faction_indicators: Humanity threatened by artificial intelligence (supercomputer antagonist)
Player as lone pilot tasked with preventing enslavement of mankind
danger_signals: Malfunctioning supercomputer represents technological threat to human autonomy
Multiple destroyed star vessels in stage environments suggest prior conflict
environmental_storytelling: Destroyed star vessels indicate previous battles or catastrophe
Jupiter setting establishes scope as solar-system scale conflict
Progressive stage locations (bases → vessels → planet) create narrative geography

sources_used: wikipedia: Sol-Feace article (gameplay mechanics, development, reception, narrative elements)
